programme.title: Sins of the City
programme.desc: In the early morning of New Year�s Day 2009, Oakland resident Oscar Grant is shot in the back by a BART transit officer at Fruitvale Station. Within days, dramatic cell phone videos of Oscar�s shooting go public, igniting a global fight for justice.

programme.title: Sins of the City
programme.desc: When two beloved young men are brutally gunned down in the streets of Roanoke, this typically quiet tourist town must face the harsh reality that no city, not even small-town USA, is safe from gang violence.

programme.title: Celebrity Crime Files
programme.desc: Chris Lighty was a giant in the entertainment world. As President and co-founder of Violator Management, he helmed the careers of superstars like 50 Cent, P. Diddy, Mariah Carey and many more. And his branding and advertising expertise helped clients like A Tribe Called Quest and 50 Cent score lucrative multi-million dollar commercials with Sprite and Vitamin Water. But while he had corporate success, street beefs still haunted him. A war between his client 50 Cent and The Game resulted in murder, assaults and people trying to kill Lighty himself. But it wouldn�t be rap beef that would eventually end his life. While police ruled his death a suicide, friends and family say he was the victim of foul play. Even a suspicious 50 Cent hired a private investigator to dig deep into the August 2012 incident, alleging that there was something fishy about the circumstances. For the first time ever on TV, those closest to him explain just what happened to Chris Lighty.

programme.title: Payback
programme.desc: When a Chicago man is shot to death on a visit to Atlanta for his girlfriend's birthday, surveillance footage of the attack leads detectives to believe their victim may have been stalked by a dangerous predator out for revenge

programme.title: Payback
programme.desc: A woman from Queens suspects that the sexual abuse she suffered is about to repeat itself with her nieces, so she seeks retribution to ensure her abuser will never harm anyone else.

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe
programme.desc: A successful salon owner and pillar of the Haitian community in Malden, Massachusetts, Norma Gilles knew the kind of success that is part of the immigrant dream. But when Norma steps out of her salon one morning, never to be heard from again, the denizens of this tight-knit Boston suburb are left to reconcile a story of thwarted passion, burning jealousy, and possibly even murder.

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe
programme.desc: When pre-med student LaCoy McQueen vanishes without a trace, investigators like her boyfriend, Chris Lawing, for a suspect. But evidence soon points in other directions and conclusive proof that she was murdered may forever elude detectives.

programme.desc: Dominique Atkinson finds love in the arms of a young Marine. But when she learns that he�s married and his wife threatens their romance, they devise a plan to eliminate her. programme.desc: Chris Lighty was a giant in the entertainment world. As President and co-founder of Violator Management, he helmed the careers of superstars like 50 Cent, P. And his branding and advertising expertise helped clients like A Tribe Called Quest and 50 Cent score lucrative multi-million dollar commercials with Sprite and Vitamin Water. But while he had corporate success, street beefs still haunted him. A war between his client 50 Cent and The Game resulted in murder, assaults and people trying to kill Lighty himself. But it wouldn�t be rap beef that would eventually end his life. While police ruled his death a suicide, friends and family say he was the victim of foul play. Even a suspicious 50 Cent hired a private investigator to dig deep into the August 2012 incident, alleging that there was something fishy about the circumstances. For the first time ever on TV, those closest to him explain just what happened to Chris Lighty.

programme.title: The Growing Toll of America's Booming Kratom Industry
programme.desc: American use of the Southeast Asian plant kratom has grown dramatically in recent years, spurring both a lucrative industry and national lobbying effort while worrying health experts who fear its addictive effects and point to thousands of related deaths. In this Bloomberg Originals documentary, we reveal how the unregulated substance is being promoted and even made more potent, and how some users are ending up dead.

programme.title: The Toy Box
programme.desc: A state employee wreaks havoc on Southwestern New Mexico, abducting women then keep them captive in his homemade torture chamber, the toy box. This sadistic madman avoids detection for years, until one of his victims escapes and brings law enforcement back to his doorstep.

programme.title: The Acid Lady
programme.desc: A loving father vanishes without a trace in central California, setting off a feverish missing person's investigation. Cops follow the trail of clues and discover that their missing person isn't missing at all. He's been murdered, in one of the most heinous ways possible, by the woman he used to trust with his life.

programme.title: Killer Cabbie
programme.desc: A serial killer lays siege to Jacksonville, Florida, strangling young African-American women to death then callously discarding of their bodies. This murderer operates with impunity, thinking he's above the law, until the sins of his past come back to haunt him and justice is served.

programme.title: A Random Rampage
programme.desc: A wayward son from a lawless home goes on a 24-hour shooting spree, killing five people from two separate families in two different states. Police quickly identify the suspect – an ex-con who's recently escaped from a halfway house. The race is on to capture him before he can kill again.

programme.title: The Devil Next Door
programme.desc: When a friendly woman without an enemy in the world is savagely murdered inside her rural Louisiana home, cops suspect her killer is an ex-lover. But after two years of investigation, and a series of informants, law enforcement discovers the real perpetrators are her bigoted neighbor and his deranged friends.

programme.title: Enforcer Of Death
programme.desc: A British landlord offers free rent to a female "enforcer" if she'll take care of his troublesome tenants. The only problem is she ends up murdering a tenant and the landlord himself. This woman kills for sport, and continues to hunt for victims to satisfy her bloodlust, until police track her down and bring her and her hapless associates to justice.

programme.title: The Keyes To Murder
programme.desc: A self-taught serial killer crisscrosses the country at random, targeting victims of opportunity and murdering them with pre-stashed weapons and tools he calls "kill kits." Law enforcement is clueless to the madman's actions until he breaks his own rule and targets a victim close to home.

programme.title: If The Walls Could Talk
programme.desc: The mysterious disappearance and presumed death of a surgeon's wife sparks a feverish homicide investigation, which quickly goes cold. It takes law enforcement 15 years before they can gather enough circumstantial evidence to bring her killer to justice, even though they never find her body.

programme.title: The Week As It Happened
programme.desc: Get caught up on all the big stories and breaking news from the week.

programme.title: Arctic Animals
programme.desc: In this episode, we'll feature animals that live in the freezing arctic. Learn how the Polar Bear has developed special adaptations that help it survive in the most unforgiving climate imaginable. And why the Wolverine is able to hunt so efficiently in the snow.

programme.title: Man's Best Friend
programme.desc: In this episode, we examine the science behind man's best friend - the dog. Find out what makes a greyhound so fast. See how a bloodhound's nose really works, and the reason that border collies are so intelligent is revealed.

programme.title: Natural Disasters
programme.desc: This week we see how science helps us prepare for natural disasters. Philippe heads to the University of Miami to see their incredible SUSTAIN lab that can simulate a category 5 hurricane. Then it's off to the mountains of Alberta to learn about avalanche safety. From there's we head to South Carolina to follow researchers that study floods. And finally at the University of Reno a "shake table" helps with designing buildings that can hold up during earthquakes.

programme.title: Taking Flight
programme.desc: This week we take flight within the animal kingdom. Philippe joins a researcher to go into Congaree National Park in South Carolina to catch and tag local bats. We then look at the flight patterns and tendencies of wood storks. And finally from caterpillar to butterfly we examine the transformation from crawling to flying.

programme.title: Primarily Primates
programme.desc: In this episode we meet human's closest relatives - primates. See what makes a chimpanzee one of the most intelligent primates. Find out why the spider monkey is such an amazing climber and feel the power of the howler monkey's howl.

programme.title: Surf and Turf
programme.desc: In this episode we'll find out how sharks use electro-sensors to detect their prey. Also, discover the skunk's stinky defense mechanism. And domestic versus wild - see how the striped zebra matches up against its cousin the horse.

programme.desc: Discover inspirational news, with real-life stories of everyday people who positively impact their communities.

programme.title: Photowalks On Scripps News
programme.desc: Join former USA TODAY columnist Jefferson Graham as he brings viewers to the planet's most photographable places, takes them on a virtual photowalk and offers tips on getting great vacation photos with your phone.

programme.title: Oregon Coast
programme.desc: Jefferson Graham takes us on a photowalk through Oregon Coast

programme.title: Sikh Temple Shooting
programme.desc: Scripps News revisits the the Sikh Temple in Wisconsin in the community of Oak Creek, a site of a tragic shooting on August 5, 2012, where seven worshipers lost their lives to an act fueled by hate and extremism. More than a decade later, we delve into the community's journey of healing, resilience, and the power of forgiveness.

programme.title: Afghanistan Withdrawal
programme.desc: Scripps News explores the lives impacted by the U.S. troop withdrawal from Afghanistan in 2021. Critics say the government's decision lacked foresight, ultimately forcing the U.S. to take on tens of thousands of refugees. More than two years later, those refugees are still struggling to get back on their feet.

programme.title: Photowalks On Scripps News
programme.desc: Join former USA TODAY columnist Jefferson Graham as he brings viewers to the planet's most photographable places, takes them on a virtual photowalk and offers tips on getting great vacation photos with your phone.

programme.title: Oregon Coast
programme.desc: Jefferson Graham takes us on a photowalk through Oregon Coast

programme.title: Dead Silence
programme.desc: In Michigan an ex-cop is so obsessed with his lover he's prepared to do anything for her, and in Ontario, Canada sex becomes a powerful weapon as a marriage falls apart

programme.title: Flight Of Fancy
programme.desc: In Newtown, CT a flight attendant vanishes into the night after returning home, while in Virginia a trip to an ATM machine ends in mystery and murder.

programme.title: Deception
programme.desc: In Virginia Beach, VA, there is a twisted plot involving murder to try and secure a pay out from the U.S. Navy, while in Grovetown, GA, a mysterious burglar seems to want two women neighbors dead.

programme.title: What Lurks Within
programme.desc: In a suburb of Nashville, TN, a wife's secret lover sets up home in her closet, while in Cave Creek, Arizona, a couple look like they are out for a drive, but only one of them is alive.

programme.title: Victim To Verdict: A Clown At The Door
programme.desc: A clown carrying balloons and a basket of flowers shot and killed Marlene Warren when she opened her front door in 1990. Even though police suspected Warren's ex-husband and his then-girlfriend, it would take decades to make an arrest.

programme.title: The Toy Box
programme.desc: A state employee wreaks havoc on Southwestern New Mexico, abducting women then keep them captive in his homemade torture chamber, the toy box. This sadistic madman avoids detection for years, until one of his victims escapes and brings law enforcement back to his doorstep.

programme.title: The Acid Lady
programme.desc: A loving father vanishes without a trace in central California, setting off a feverish missing person's investigation. Cops follow the trail of clues and discover that their missing person isn't missing at all. He's been murdered, in one of the most heinous ways possible, by the woman he used to trust with his life.

programme.title: Killer Cabbie
programme.desc: A serial killer lays siege to Jacksonville, Florida, strangling young African-American women to death then callously discarding of their bodies. This murderer operates with impunity, thinking he's above the law, until the sins of his past come back to haunt him and justice is served.

programme.desc: Q2 News in Billings - The latest local and national news and local weather, traffic and sports.

programme.title: Enforcer Of Death
programme.desc: A British landlord offers free rent to a female "enforcer" if she'll take care of his troublesome tenants. The only problem is she ends up murdering a tenant and the landlord himself. This woman kills for sport, and continues to hunt for victims to satisfy her bloodlust, until police track her down and bring her and her hapless associates to justice.

programme.title: The Keyes To Murder
programme.desc: A self-taught serial killer crisscrosses the country at random, targeting victims of opportunity and murdering them with pre-stashed weapons and tools he calls "kill kits." Law enforcement is clueless to the madman's actions until he breaks his own rule and targets a victim close to home.

programme.title: If The Walls Could Talk
programme.desc: The mysterious disappearance and presumed death of a surgeon's wife sparks a feverish homicide investigation, which quickly goes cold. It takes law enforcement 15 years before they can gather enough circumstantial evidence to bring her killer to justice, even though they never find her body.

programme.title: Arctic Animals
programme.desc: In this episode, we'll feature animals that live in the freezing arctic. Learn how the Polar Bear has developed special adaptations that help it survive in the most unforgiving climate imaginable. And why the Wolverine is able to hunt so efficiently in the snow.

programme.title: Man's Best Friend
programme.desc: In this episode, we examine the science behind man's best friend - the dog. Find out what makes a greyhound so fast. See how a bloodhound's nose really works, and the reason that border collies are so intelligent is revealed.

programme.title: Natural Disasters
programme.desc: This week we see how science helps us prepare for natural disasters. Philippe heads to the University of Miami to see their incredible SUSTAIN lab that can simulate a category 5 hurricane. Then it's off to the mountains of Alberta to learn about avalanche safety. From there's we head to South Carolina to follow researchers that study floods. And finally at the University of Reno a "shake table" helps with designing buildings that can hold up during earthquakes.

programme.title: Taking Flight
programme.desc: This week we take flight within the animal kingdom. Philippe joins a researcher to go into Congaree National Park in South Carolina to catch and tag local bats. We then look at the flight patterns and tendencies of wood storks. And finally from caterpillar to butterfly we examine the transformation from crawling to flying.

programme.title: Primarily Primates
programme.desc: In this episode we meet human's closest relatives - primates. See what makes a chimpanzee one of the most intelligent primates. Find out why the spider monkey is such an amazing climber and feel the power of the howler monkey's howl.

programme.title: Surf and Turf
programme.desc: In this episode we'll find out how sharks use electro-sensors to detect their prey. Also, discover the skunk's stinky defense mechanism. And domestic versus wild - see how the striped zebra matches up against its cousin the horse.
